goal: Yrkesroll: Assembler / assembly worker (ONET SOC 51-2099, Assemblers and Fabricators, All Other) at a production peg-into-bushing insertion station. A Franka Panda performs the everyday hands-on cadence of an assembly-line operator: pick a cylindrical peg from an infeed tray, transit to the bushing fixture clamped to the worktable, descend under cartesian impedance with a 20 N Z-axis force cap, release inside the bushing bore, and repeat for THREE pegs in sequence. Success is measured at the production-cadence level as a SUCCESS RATE across the 3-peg run: number of pegs whose final centre is within 3 mm of the bushing axis and whose final Z is below the seated-threshold (0.815 m), divided by 3. Realizes backlog entry yrkesroll-assembler-peg-bushing-001. Distinct from CP-NEW-peg-bushing-impedance (A14: single-peg illustrative impedance demonstration, no production-cadence framing) and CP-NEW-maniskill-peg-insertion (A101: ManiSkill benchmark eval format, vertical, no pick phase, peg pre-grasped, single trial) — this canonical is the YRKESROLL framing of the peg-into-bushing pattern: full pick + transit + insert flow per peg, 3-peg sequential cadence, success-rate metric, operator-role metadata block tying back to ONET SOC 51-2099.
thoughts: Yrkesroll framing — Assembler / assembly worker (ONET SOC 51-2099, Assemblers and Fabricators, All Other). Backlog entry yrkesroll-assembler-peg-bushing-001 calls out 'Franka inserts a cylindrical peg into a bushing using impedance control; insertion force capped at 20 N'. The single-peg shape is already covered by A14 (CP-NEW-peg-bushing-impedance, illustrative impedance) and A101 (CP-NEW-maniskill-peg-insertion, benchmark eval). This canonical fills the missing slot: the YRKESROLL cadence — what an actual production assembler does at the station, namely a sequence of pegs, each going through pick + transit + insert, judged by a production success rate rather than by a single-trial outcome.

Distinction from A14 (CP-NEW-peg-bushing-impedance, the closest sibling): A14 is single-peg, table-top, impedance-illustration. Its goal sentence is 'inserts a cylindrical peg into a bushing fixture using impedance control with a force/torque sensor capping insertion force at 20 N'. It demonstrates the impedance + assembly-constraint pattern but has no production-cadence framing, no operator-role metadata block, and no success-rate metric — just a single-trial settle_state. A107 inherits the impedance + force-cap pattern from A14 but adds: (1) 3-peg infeed tray, (2) sequential pick+transit+insert loop, (3) operator_role metadata block tying to ONET SOC 51-2099, (4) production_metrics.success_rate, (5) yrkesroll structural tags.

Distinction from A101 (CP-NEW-maniskill-peg-insertion, the benchmark sibling): A101 is benchmark-shaped — peg pre-grasped, no infeed, no pick phase, pure compliance descent only, eval format = ManiSkill PegInsertionSide-v0 metric dict (success_rate, success_at_depth_threshold, peg_seated, contact_observed). A107 is yrkesroll-shaped — pegs ungrasped on infeed tray, full pick + transit + insert per peg, eval format = production_metrics.success_rate (count of pegs seated / 3), operator_role metadata for ONET SOC 51-2099. A101's success_rate is policy-rollout-over-many-episodes; A107's success_rate is one-pass-three-pegs-at-this-station-on-this-shift.

Topology — single station on a 1.0×0.6×0.75m worktable. Franka at [0, 0, 0.75]. Bushing fixture (Cube housing with implied Cylinder bore) pinned to the table at [0.0, -0.25, 0.795], scale [0.10, 0.10, 0.04] = 20cm x 20cm x 8cm fixture block; bushing bore top rim at z = 0.815. Infeed tray (Cube pallet) at [0.30, 0.10, 0.755], scale [0.20, 0.15, 0.005], holding 3 pegs in a row along the +Y axis at y=0.05, 0.10, 0.15. Each peg is a Cylinder of radius 0.012m, height 0.07m. Per-peg start positions: Peg_1 [0.30, 0.05, 0.815], Peg_2 [0.30, 0.10, 0.815], Peg_3 [0.30, 0.15, 0.815] (peg centre z = tray top + half-height = 0.7575 + 0.035 = approx 0.815).

Bushing geometry — modeled as a SOLID Cube fixture with the bore approximated by a slightly-larger-than-peg cylindrical landing volume. Real bushing has a hollow bore; we land each peg on the top rim of the bushing fixture and let the impedance Z-soft surrender settle the peg into the bore opening (assembly-constraint with 3 mm tolerance captures the seated state). Peg radius 0.012 m, bushing bore implied radius 0.015 m (3 mm tolerance), so the peg has 3 mm radial slack on entry. This is the same pattern A14 uses but parameterised per-peg via the workpieces[] role.

Production cadence — 3 pegs processed in sequence. Each peg has its own pick + transit + insert phase. Inter-peg, the robot returns to a neutral home pose. The 3 pegs are processed in row order along +Y so cuRobo's planning_obstacles list does not need to update between pegs (each peg's source is distinct, no peg blocks another). Total wall-clock budget at velocity_scaling=0.15 is approximately 60-80 s for 3 pegs in dry_run mode (no actual physics propagation). simulate_args.duration_s=240 budgets a 4-minute envelope for the live (non-dry-run) cycle.

Compliance profile per insertion — cartesian_impedance with Kx=[300, 300, 80] N/m (X/Y stiff to keep peg axially aligned with bore centre, Z soft so the controller surrenders to the 20 N cap), Kr=[30, 30, 30] N·m/rad (all rotation stiff, no twist needed for peg-into-round-bore). Damping Dx=[30, 30, 10], Dr=[3, 3, 3]. Same profile values as A14, applied identically to each of the 3 inserts. The impedance controller is INSTALLED ONCE before peg 1; subsequent pegs reuse the active compliance state. This is the production cadence — the operator does not re-tune the wrist between pegs.

Force cap 20 N — inherited from backlog spec and from A14. Applied via add_force_torque_sensor with threshold=20.0 on /World/Franka/FtSensor parented at panda_hand. Each insert phase checks the sensor between trajectory waypoints; on breach, the impedance controller's Z-soft compliance absorbs the overshoot. Same value as A14, half of A91's snap-fit 25 N (snap-fit has 4 prongs sharing load, peg has 1).

Phase ordering per peg — PHASE 1 (transit): setup_pick_place_controller picks peg_i from infeed tray, places directly above bushing at z=0.835 (20 mm above the bore rim, ready for the descent). PHASE 2 (press): follow_trajectory_with_compliance executes the 3-waypoint descent: hover z=0.835 -> rim contact z=0.820 -> seated z=0.810. compliance_handoff_at=0.33 so the rim-contact + seated waypoints run under cartesian_impedance while the hover-to-rim segment runs under cuRobo. PHASE 3 (verify): get_world_transform on peg_i confirms final position within 3 mm radial tolerance and final Z below 0.815 seated-threshold. PHASE 4: increment i, repeat until 3 pegs done.

Success-rate metric — production_metrics.success_rate = (number of pegs whose final centre is within radial_tolerance_m=0.003 of bushing axis AND final Z below seated_z_max_m=0.815) / 3. expected_success_rate=1.0 for a working pipeline; expected_success_rate>=0.67 (2-of-3) for a degraded pipeline. This is distinct from A101's ManiSkill-format success_rate (policy rollout aggregate over many episodes) — A107's success_rate is per-shift, per-station, 3-peg cadence.

motion_controllers honesty: curobo via setup_pick_place_controller for the pick + transit phase (untested), cartesian_impedance via follow_trajectory_with_compliance for the insert phase (untested). Same as A14 which used curobo + impedance. No verified runs for production cadence — production cadence requires a ros2_control bridge for live impedance and physics live for 3 sequential pegs without drift; both are pending function-gate sign-off.
verified_status: drafted on 2026-05-16; form-gate-schema pending lint; function-gate pending Kit RPC + ros2_control bridge for live impedance press + live 3-peg cadence.
code:
# ── Scene skeleton ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
create_prim(prim_path="/World/DomeLight", prim_type="DomeLight")
set_attribute(prim_path="/World/DomeLight", attr_name="inputs:intensity", value=1000.0)
create_prim(prim_path="/World/Ground", prim_type="Cube", position=[0, 0, -0.5], scale=[20, 20, 1])
apply_api_schema(prim_path="/World/Ground", schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")

# Physics: CPU deterministic for reproducible 3-peg cadence.
set_physics_scene_config(config={"enable_gpu_dynamics": False, "broadphase_type": "MBP"})

# Worktable
create_prim(prim_path="/World/Table", prim_type="Cube", position=[0, 0, 0.375], scale=[1.0, 0.6, 0.375])
apply_api_schema(prim_path="/World/Table", schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")

# ── Robot ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
robot_wizard(
    robot_name="franka_panda",
    dest_path="/World/Franka",
    position=[0, 0, 0.75],
    orientation=[0.7071068, 0, 0, 0.7071068],
)

# ── Bushing fixture (Cube housing block, static, pinned by absence of RigidBodyAPI) ──
# Bore top rim z = 0.795 + 0.04/2 = 0.815. Peg seated centre z = 0.810 (5 mm below rim).
create_prim(
    prim_path="/World/Bushing",
    prim_type="Cube",
    position=[0.0, -0.25, 0.795],
    scale=[0.10, 0.10, 0.04],
)
apply_api_schema(prim_path="/World/Bushing", schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")
set_attribute(prim_path="/World/Bushing", attr_name="primvars:displayColor", value=[[0.40, 0.30, 0.20]])

# ── Infeed tray (production-cadence pallet, 3 pegs in a row) ─────────────────
create_prim(
    prim_path="/World/InfeedTray",
    prim_type="Cube",
    position=[0.30, 0.10, 0.755],
    scale=[0.20, 0.15, 0.005],
)
apply_api_schema(prim_path="/World/InfeedTray", schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")
set_attribute(prim_path="/World/InfeedTray", attr_name="primvars:displayColor", value=[[0.55, 0.55, 0.55]])

# ── Three pegs on the infeed tray (production batch) ─────────────────────────
# Each peg: Cylinder r=0.012, h=0.07. Centre z = tray top (0.7575) + h/2 (0.035) = 0.7925.
# Pegs spaced along +Y axis at y = 0.05, 0.10, 0.15 within the tray footprint.
peg_positions = [
    [0.30, 0.05, 0.7925],
    [0.30, 0.10, 0.7925],
    [0.30, 0.15, 0.7925],
]
peg_paths = []
for idx, pos in enumerate(peg_positions):
    peg_path = f"/World/Peg_{idx + 1}"
    peg_paths.append(peg_path)
    create_prim(
        prim_path=peg_path,
        prim_type="Cylinder",
        position=pos,
        radius=0.012,
        height=0.07,
    )
    for api in ("PhysicsRigidBodyAPI", "PhysicsCollisionAPI", "PhysicsMassAPI", "PhysxRigidBodyAPI"):
        apply_api_schema(prim_path=peg_path, schema_name=api)
    set_attribute(prim_path=peg_path, attr_name="physics:mass", value=0.025)
    set_attribute(prim_path=peg_path, attr_name="primvars:displayColor", value=[[0.20, 0.50, 0.65]])
    apply_physics_material(prim_path=peg_path, material_name="rubber")

# Sleep-threshold zero on pegs so the press signal is not lost to sleep.
bulk_set_attribute(
    prim_paths=peg_paths,
    attr="physxRigidBody:sleepThreshold",
    value=0.0,
)
bulk_set_attribute(
    prim_paths=peg_paths + ["/World/Bushing"],
    attr="physxRigidBody:solverPositionIterationCount",
    value=16,
)

# ── Force / torque sensor — 20 N insertion force cap (shared across all 3 pegs) ─
add_force_torque_sensor(
    sensor_path="/World/Franka/FtSensor",
    parent_path="/World/Franka/panda_hand",
    threshold=20.0,
    noise_std=0.05,
)

# ── Proximity sensor for pick detection (covers all 3 peg slots on the tray) ──
add_proximity_sensor(
    sensor_path="/World/PickSensor",
    position=[0.30, 0.10, 0.815],
    size=[0.20, 0.15, 0.10],
)

# ── Impedance controller — installed ONCE, reused across all 3 pegs ──────────
# Kx=[300, 300, 80] — X/Y rigid centering, Z soft for 20 N descent.
# Kr=[30, 30, 30] — all rotation stiff (peg-into-round-bore: no twist needed).
setup_impedance_controller(
    robot_path="/World/Franka",
    target_frame="panda_hand",
    Kx=[300.0, 300.0, 80.0],
    Kr=[30.0, 30.0, 30.0],
    Dx=[30.0, 30.0, 10.0],
    Dr=[3.0, 3.0, 3.0],
    null_space_stiffness=0.5,
    null_space_damping=0.5,
    torque_mode=True,
    dry_run=True,
)
set_compliance_params(
    robot_path="/World/Franka",
    stiffness_xyz=[300.0, 300.0, 80.0],
    damping_xyz=[30.0, 30.0, 10.0],
    stiffness_rot=[30.0, 30.0, 30.0],
    damping_rot=[3.0, 3.0, 3.0],
    dry_run=True,
)

# ── PRODUCTION CADENCE: 3 pegs in sequence, each pick + transit + insert ─────
# Peg 1 of 3 ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
define_grasp_pose(
    robot_path="/World/Franka",
    object_path="/World/Peg_1",
    approach_direction=[0, 0, -1],
    gripper_offset=[0, 0, 0],
)
setup_pick_place_controller(
    robot_path="/World/Franka",
    target_source="curobo",
    sensor_path="/World/PickSensor",
    source_paths=["/World/Peg_1"],
    destination_path="/World/Bushing",
    drop_target=[0.0, -0.25, 0.835],
    planning_obstacles=["/World/Table", "/World/Bushing", "/World/InfeedTray"],
    approach_height=0.12,
    lift_height=0.12,
)
follow_trajectory_with_compliance(
    trajectory=[
        {"pose": {"position": [0.0, -0.25, 0.835], "orientation": [1.0, 0.0, 0.0, 0.0]}},
        {"pose": {"position": [0.0, -0.25, 0.820], "orientation": [1.0, 0.0, 0.0, 0.0]}},
        {"pose": {"position": [0.0, -0.25, 0.810], "orientation": [1.0, 0.0, 0.0, 0.0]}}
    ],
    robot_path="/World/Franka",
    compliance_handoff_at=0.33,
    compliance_controller="cartesian_impedance",
    timeout_s=15.0,
    velocity_scaling=0.15,
    dry_run=True,
)
get_joint_torques(articulation="/World/Franka")
get_world_transform(prim_path="/World/Peg_1")

# Peg 2 of 3 ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
define_grasp_pose(
    robot_path="/World/Franka",
    object_path="/World/Peg_2",
    approach_direction=[0, 0, -1],
    gripper_offset=[0, 0, 0],
)
setup_pick_place_controller(
    robot_path="/World/Franka",
    target_source="curobo",
    sensor_path="/World/PickSensor",
    source_paths=["/World/Peg_2"],
    destination_path="/World/Bushing",
    drop_target=[0.0, -0.25, 0.835],
    planning_obstacles=["/World/Table", "/World/Bushing", "/World/InfeedTray"],
    approach_height=0.12,
    lift_height=0.12,
)
follow_trajectory_with_compliance(
    trajectory=[
        {"pose": {"position": [0.0, -0.25, 0.835], "orientation": [1.0, 0.0, 0.0, 0.0]}},
        {"pose": {"position": [0.0, -0.25, 0.820], "orientation": [1.0, 0.0, 0.0, 0.0]}},
        {"pose": {"position": [0.0, -0.25, 0.810], "orientation": [1.0, 0.0, 0.0, 0.0]}}
    ],
    robot_path="/World/Franka",
    compliance_handoff_at=0.33,
    compliance_controller="cartesian_impedance",
    timeout_s=15.0,
    velocity_scaling=0.15,
    dry_run=True,
)
get_joint_torques(articulation="/World/Franka")
get_world_transform(prim_path="/World/Peg_2")

# Peg 3 of 3 ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
define_grasp_pose(
    robot_path="/World/Franka",
    object_path="/World/Peg_3",
    approach_direction=[0, 0, -1],
    gripper_offset=[0, 0, 0],
)
setup_pick_place_controller(
    robot_path="/World/Franka",
    target_source="curobo",
    sensor_path="/World/PickSensor",
    source_paths=["/World/Peg_3"],
    destination_path="/World/Bushing",
    drop_target=[0.0, -0.25, 0.835],
    planning_obstacles=["/World/Table", "/World/Bushing", "/World/InfeedTray"],
    approach_height=0.12,
    lift_height=0.12,
)
follow_trajectory_with_compliance(
    trajectory=[
        {"pose": {"position": [0.0, -0.25, 0.835], "orientation": [1.0, 0.0, 0.0, 0.0]}},
        {"pose": {"position": [0.0, -0.25, 0.820], "orientation": [1.0, 0.0, 0.0, 0.0]}},
        {"pose": {"position": [0.0, -0.25, 0.810], "orientation": [1.0, 0.0, 0.0, 0.0]}}
    ],
    robot_path="/World/Franka",
    compliance_handoff_at=0.33,
    compliance_controller="cartesian_impedance",
    timeout_s=15.0,
    velocity_scaling=0.15,
    dry_run=True,
)
get_joint_torques(articulation="/World/Franka")
get_world_transform(prim_path="/World/Peg_3")
extension_notes: Implements ONET SOC 51-2099 assembler persona (Assemblers and Fabricators, All Other) at a production peg-into-bushing insertion station. Backlog alignment: extends yrkesroll-assembler-peg-bushing-001 (config/canonical_backlog.yaml). Key differentiators: (1) PRODUCTION-CADENCE framing — 3 pegs in sequence at one station, not single-trial. (2) Operator-role metadata block tying to ONET SOC 51-2099 (same code as A91 snap-fit; the assembler persona spans multiple insertion patterns at the same workstation grade). (3) production_metrics.success_rate = (pegs_seated_within_tolerance / 3) — distinct from A101's ManiSkill-format success_rate (policy rollout aggregate over many episodes) and from A14's single-trial settle_state (no success-rate metric). (4) Full pick + transit + insert flow PER peg — distinct from A101 which is pre-grasped and pure-descent. (5) Inherits impedance + force-cap pattern (Kx=[300,300,80], 20 N cap) from A14 but applies it across the 3-peg cadence with the impedance controller installed ONCE and reused. (6) verify_args.stages encodes the 3 sequential pick-place phases (one per peg) so the canonical evaluator runs the cadence end-to-end. compliance_handoff_at=0.33 splits each insert trajectory into a cuRobo hover-to-rim segment and a cartesian_impedance rim-to-seated descent — same handoff pattern used by CRM-B1 canonicals. PhysX numerical instability during peg grip is a shared risk with all impedance-press canonicals (see A14 failure mode 1).
failure_modes: PhysX numerical instability across 3-peg cadence: peg velocity spikes >200 km/s during Franka grip — same shared risk as A14 and A101 single-peg cases, but ACCUMULATES over 3 inserts because the impedance controller stays installed across pegs and any solver state drift in peg N is carried into peg N+1. Mitigation: solver_position_iteration_count=16 (applied via bulk_set_attribute), rubber material on pegs, sleepThreshold=0 to prevent the awake/asleep oscillation that triggers velocity blow-up. Production rollout should periodically reset the solver between pegs (not modeled here — dry_run only).

Bushing is solid Cube fixture with no actual hollow bore — peg lands on the fixture's top face, not inside a real bore; assembly_constraint not declared (unlike A14 which uses setup_assembly_constraint with tolerance=0.003). The seated state is approximated by the peg's final centre Z being below 0.815. Real production bushing needs mesh import (add_usd_reference of a bushing OBJ/USD with internal cylindrical hollow) or CreateDeformableMesh. Failure mode shared with A14 but more visible here because the metric (success_rate) is computed from peg final-Z.

drop_target Z=0.835 + descent waypoints [0.835, 0.820, 0.810] require cuRobo IK reachability at exactly those coordinates for each of the 3 pegs (same drop_target across pegs because all 3 go into the same bushing). If cuRobo IK fails for the seated z=0.810 on any peg, that peg's insert phase stalls and its final-Z lookup returns the stalled position (above seated threshold) — success_rate drops to <1.0. Mitigation: solve_ik pre-flight at [0.0, -0.25, 0.810] before installing the impedance controller.

setup_impedance_controller and set_compliance_params are dry_run=True — no live torque-mode control without a ros2_control bridge; production rollout requires setup_ros2_control_compat first. Shared risk with all CRM-B1 canonicals (A14 peg-bushing, A91 snap-fit, screw-driving, bottle-cap-tighten).

follow_trajectory_with_compliance is Phase 63b — pose-only waypoints accepted but cuRobo replan is gated. compliance_handoff_at=0.33 means waypoints 2 and 3 (rim-contact, seated) run under cartesian_impedance while waypoint 1 (hover) runs under cuRobo. The bridge accepts this configuration but emits no handoff_mismatch_warning. Same risk shape as A91 snap-fit.

Sequential peg processing has no inter-peg home-pose return — the robot moves directly from peg N's seated waypoint to peg N+1's pick approach. If cuRobo's plan from a seated wrist-down pose at [0, -0.25, 0.810] to the next pick at [0.30, y_{N+1}, 0.835] hits the InfeedTray or Bushing as a planning obstacle, the transit phase stalls. Mitigation for production: insert an explicit home-pose move_to_pose between pegs (not modeled here to keep the canonical's tool count manageable and the production-cadence story uninterrupted).

Force cap 20 N applied via add_force_torque_sensor threshold=20.0 — the sensor reports breach but does NOT auto-pause the trajectory. In dry_run mode this is documentation-only; in production, the consumer must poll get_joint_torques between waypoints and abort the trajectory if any reading exceeds 20 N. Modeled here by the get_joint_torques call inserted after each insert phase as a post-hoc check.

Pegs are placed on the infeed tray with no PhysicsFixedJoint or attach_to_articulation binding — they sit on the tray surface held by gravity + friction (rubber material). If physics settles them outside the radial pick tolerance of setup_pick_place_controller (typical 0.005-0.010 m), the controller misses the grasp. Mitigation: define_grasp_pose is called per peg to refresh the grasp target right before each pick, but if a peg has rolled, the refresh sees the rolled pose; production rollout would need a vision pose-update step (vision_detect_objects).

production_metrics.success_rate of 1.0 is the EXPECTED rate for a working pipeline — the canonical does not yet have a verified run, so this is an aspirational metric, not a measured one (see verified_status). Downstream consumers should treat success_rate as a CONTRACT (what the canonical claims), not a measurement (what the canonical achieves). The form-gate-schema lint pass validates the contract shape; the function-gate would validate the achieved rate.
verified_status: drafted-2026-05-16; form-gate-schema pending lint; function-gate pending Kit RPC + ros2_control bridge + 3-peg live cadence

workpieces.note: Three pegs processed in row order along +Y axis on the infeed tray; one production cadence at the station.

operator_role.title: Assembler / Assembly Worker (Production-Line)
operator_role.onet_soc_note: 51-2099 is the broad assembly-worker code, shared with A91 snap-fit (the assembler persona spans multiple insertion patterns at the same workstation grade — peg-into-bushing here, snap-fit clamshell in A91). Adjacent code 51-2028 from the older 2018 SOC revision is also used in older NIST references; we use 51-2099 to match the 2018-2028 SOC structure as of 2026.

code_template:
create_prim(prim_path="/World/DomeLight", prim_type="DomeLight")
set_attribute(prim_path="/World/DomeLight", attr_name="inputs:intensity", value=1000.0)
create_prim(prim_path="/World/Ground", prim_type="Cube", position=[0, 0, -0.5], scale=[20, 20, 1])
apply_api_schema(prim_path="/World/Ground", schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")
set_physics_scene_config(config={"enable_gpu_dynamics": False, "broadphase_type": "MBP"})
create_prim(prim_path="/World/Table", prim_type="Cube", position=[0, 0, 0.375], scale=[1.0, 0.6, 0.375])
apply_api_schema(prim_path="/World/Table", schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")
robot_wizard(robot_name={{primary_robot.class}}, dest_path={{primary_robot.path}}, position={{primary_robot.position}}, orientation={{primary_robot.orientation}})
create_prim(prim_path={{bushing.path}}, prim_type="Cube", position={{bushing.position}}, scale={{bushing.scale}})
apply_api_schema(prim_path={{bushing.path}}, schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")
create_prim(prim_path={{infeed_tray.path}}, prim_type="Cube", position={{infeed_tray.position}}, scale={{infeed_tray.scale}})
apply_api_schema(prim_path={{infeed_tray.path}}, schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")
create_prim(prim_path={{workpieces[0].path}}, prim_type="Cylinder", position={{workpieces[0].position}}, radius={{workpieces[0].radius}}, height={{workpieces[0].height}})
for api in ("PhysicsRigidBodyAPI", "PhysicsCollisionAPI", "PhysicsMassAPI", "PhysxRigidBodyAPI"):
    apply_api_schema(prim_path={{workpieces[0].path}}, schema_name=api)
set_attribute(prim_path={{workpieces[0].path}}, attr_name="physics:mass", value={{workpieces[0].mass}})
apply_physics_material(prim_path={{workpieces[0].path}}, material_name={{workpieces[0].material}})
create_prim(prim_path={{workpieces[1].path}}, prim_type="Cylinder", position={{workpieces[1].position}}, radius={{workpieces[1].radius}}, height={{workpieces[1].height}})
for api in ("PhysicsRigidBodyAPI", "PhysicsCollisionAPI", "PhysicsMassAPI", "PhysxRigidBodyAPI"):
    apply_api_schema(prim_path={{workpieces[1].path}}, schema_name=api)
set_attribute(prim_path={{workpieces[1].path}}, attr_name="physics:mass", value={{workpieces[1].mass}})
